components.name: Debate Engine
components.purpose: Multi-model spec critique loop with CLI orchestration

components.name: Gauntlet
components.purpose: 7-phase adversarial stress-testing with named attacker personas

components.name: Providers
components.purpose: Model config, credentials, cost rates, Bedrock support

components.name: MCP Tasks
components.purpose: Cross-agent task coordination via MCP protocol

warnings: execution_planner/ deprecation Phase 3 (cleanup exports) still PENDING
scope.py (606 lines) is not imported by any module — potential dead code
knowledge_service.py implemented but not wired into any active flow

hazards.description: Multiple threads call cost_tracker.add() simultaneously. Dict updates to by_model not atomic. Currently safe under GIL but Python 3.14+ PEP 703 may remove GIL. Cost tracking is informational only — no business logic depends on accuracy.

patterns.description: Three CLI model functions duplicate 80+ lines each of subprocess boilerplate
patterns.concern: Maintenance risk: changes require updating 3 locations. Blocks clean addition of new CLI models.
patterns.best_practice: Extract generic _call_cli_model() function with model-specific output parser hooks

patterns.description: JSON load with try/except fallback repeated 8+ times across both files
patterns.concern: Scattered identical error handling makes uniform updates difficult

patterns.description: Env vars read via os.environ.get() scattered across 4+ files with no centralization
patterns.concern: Difficult to audit required env vars. No centralized validation.
patterns.best_practice: Create config.py with centralized getenv functions per concern area

patterns.description: Path safety check is_relative_to() repeated 4 times with identical code
patterns.concern: Security-critical logic duplicated; risk of inconsistent updates

patterns.description: All test files repeat identical sys.path.insert preamble for imports
Both files independently suppress litellm logging at import time
Both CLIs use same exit codes (1=runtime, 2=config) but convention is implicit
Both CLIs implement argparse with subparsers — framework-mandated pattern

findings.title: scope.py is dead code (606 lines, imported by nothing)
findings.description: scope.py defines ScopeDiscovery and related functions but is not imported anywhere
findings.suggestion: Integrate into debate workflow or delete
findings.evidence: grep for 'import.*scope' returns zero results across all scripts

findings.title: gauntlet.py exceeds maintainable single-file size (4087 lines)
findings.description: Contains 7-phase pipeline, data classes, evaluation logic, medal tracking, report formatting, stats persistence, and CLI
findings.suggestion: Split into gauntlet/ package: pipeline.py, evaluation.py, stats.py, report.py, types.py
findings.evidence: 4087 lines. debate.py is 1485, models.py is 937 for comparison

findings.title: cost_tracker relies on GIL for thread safety
findings.description: Global CostTracker written from multiple threads without lock. Safe under GIL, but Python 3.14+ may remove GIL (PEP 703)
findings.evidence: cost_tracker.add() at models.py:163 called from ThreadPoolExecutor threads. No lock.

findings.title: No file locking on .claude/tasks.json
findings.description: MCP server and TaskManager both read/write tasks.json without locking. Concurrent access possible.
findings.suggestion: Add fcntl.flock or use single-writer pattern
findings.evidence: load_tasks()/save_tasks() use plain path.read_text()/write_text() with no locking

findings.title: Execution planner exports still include potentially dead types
findings.description: Deprecation Phase 3 (cleanup exports) still pending per spec
findings.suggestion: Audit which exports are still imported externally, remove unused ones
findings.evidence: Deprecation spec lists Phase 3 as PENDING

findings.title: knowledge_service.py implemented but not wired into any flow
findings.description: Full caching implementation exists at integrations/knowledge_service.py but no code path calls it
findings.suggestion: Wire into pre-gauntlet flow or remove
findings.evidence: Only references are definition file and __init__.py re-export

findings.title: Model names passed to subprocess without character validation
findings.description: CLI model names from --models flag passed to subprocess command arrays without character set validation
findings.suggestion: Validate model names against allowed character set (alphanumeric, hyphens, slashes, dots) — defense-in-depth
findings.evidence: models.py:399 cmd includes model_name from CLI args. subprocess.run with list args prevents shell injection but model name could be interpreted as CLI flag.
